pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 1.8 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: The build will compile against Java 8 semantics and bytecode if this is not changed, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin version to support Java 21 compilation.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin may not support compiling source/target/release level 21, leading to build failures.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace separate source and target configuration with the unified release configuration in maven-compiler-plugin.
pom.xml.why_exists: While source/target works, release is the modern standard for Java 9+ toolchains; however, this is often considered polishment unless specific toolchain issues arise, but given the context of a major JDK upgrade, it's part of the standard migration pattern. Strictly speaking, source/target 21 works in newer plugins, so this is polishment.
